name: Salt River
ability: Provides 1 Water Energy.
Exhaust to pay Water costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: The Salt River flows through central Arizona.
It is formed by the Black and White Rivers.
The river supplies water to the Phoenix metropolitan area.

name: Tonto Creek
ability: Provides 1 Water Energy.
Exhaust to pay Water costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Tonto Creek flows from the Mogollon Rim into Roosevelt Lake, linking Arizona's pine forests to the Sonoran Desert.

name: Prickly Pear
ability: Provides 1 Flora Energy.
Exhaust to pay Flora costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Prickly pear pads store water during drought.
Its fruit is eaten by many desert animals.
The cactus provides shelter as well as food.

name: Velvet Mesquite
ability: Provides 1 Flora Energy.
Exhaust to pay Flora costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Velvet mesquite is native to the Sonoran Desert.
Its seed pods are an important wildlife food.
Deep roots allow the tree to survive long droughts.

name: Saguaro
ability: Shelter: Exhaust this card when a friendly Prey is challenged. That challenge immediately ends.
Thorns: The challenger loses 1 Challenge Rank until the end of the current challenge.
fact: Saguaros provide nesting sites for birds.
The cactus stores large amounts of water.
Only grows naturally in the Sonoran Desert.

name: Ocotillo
ability: Shelter: Exhaust this card when a friendly Prey is challenged. That challenge immediately ends.
Thorns: The challenger loses 1 Challenge Rank until the end of the current challenge.
fact: Ocotillo quickly grows leaves after rainfall.
Its flowers provide nectar for hummingbirds.
Dense thorny stems provide cover for wildlife.

name: Habitat Omnivore
ability: Provides Habitat support.
fact: TBD

name: Desert Kangaroo Rat
ability: Burrow: When challenged, you may exhaust this card to escape the challenge.
fact: Stores seeds in underground burrows.
Can survive without drinking free water.
Powerful hind legs allow rapid escape.

name: Javelina
ability: Herd Defense: This card gains +1 Challenge Rank while another Territorial card is in your Habitat.
Charge: When challenged, the attacker loses 1 Challenge Rank during this challenge.
fact: Lives in family groups called squadrons.
Defends territory aggressively.
Feeds mainly on cactus, roots, and fruit.

name: Desert Cottontail
ability: Provides 1 Fauna Energy.
Exhaust to pay Fauna costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Feeds on grasses, cactus pads and shrubs.
Most active during dawn and dusk.
A common prey species of the Sonoran Desert.

name: Gambel's Quail
ability: Provides 1 Fauna Energy.
Exhaust to pay Fauna costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Lives in family groups called coveys.
Feeds mainly on seeds and desert plants.
Arizona's state bird.

name: Cactus Wren
ability: Nest: If this card occupies a Saguaro or Ocotillo, it gains +1 Challenge Rank.
Flight: This card may escape one challenge each turn.
fact: Builds nests in thorny desert plants.
Arizona's state bird.
Uses cactus spines for protection.

name: Ringtail
ability: Scavenge: Once per turn, reduce the Fauna cost of the next card you play by 1.
fact: Arizona's state mammal.
Eats fruit, insects, rodents and birds.
Excellent climber with a long striped tail.

name: Gray Fox
ability: Adaptable: This card may use either Flora or Fauna to pay 1 cost when played.
fact: One of the few canines that regularly climbs trees.
Eats fruit, insects and small animals.
Common throughout the Sonoran Desert.

name: Common Raven
ability: Scout: Look at the top card of your deck. You may leave it or place it on the bottom.
fact: Highly intelligent and capable of solving problems.
Feeds on carrion, insects, seeds and fruit.
Often follows predators to find food.

name: Skunk
ability: Spray: The first Predator to challenge this card loses 1 Challenge Rank until end of turn.
fact: Uses a strong defensive spray to deter predators.
Eats insects, fruit, eggs and small animals.
Mostly active at night.

name: Western Diamondback
ability: Challenge a Prey or Territorial Organism.
If this challenge succeeds, gain 1 Fauna Energy.
fact: The western diamondback is the largest rattlesnake in the Sonoran Desert.
It uses heat-sensing pits to detect warm-blooded prey.

name: Coachwhip
ability: Challenge a Prey Organism.
May immediately challenge again if the first challenge succeeds.
fact: Coachwhips are among the fastest snakes in North America.
They actively chase prey instead of waiting in ambush.

name: Gila Monster
ability: Challenge a Prey Organism.
Defending abilities cannot prevent this challenge.
fact: The Gila monster is one of the few venomous lizards in the world.
It stores fat in its tail, allowing it to survive long periods without food.

name: Great Horned Owl
ability: Challenge a Prey Organism.
May ignore Shelter abilities during a challenge.
fact: Great horned owls hunt primarily at night.
Their powerful talons can exert hundreds of pounds of force.

name: Osprey
ability: Challenge an Organism near Water.
Costs 1 less Water to play if a Water Energy is in play.
fact: Ospreys dive feet-first into water to catch fish.
Their reversible outer toe helps grip slippery prey.

name: Turkey Vulture
ability: May Compost one defeated Organism to gain 1 Fauna Energy.
Cannot initiate a challenge.
fact: Turkey vultures locate carrion using an exceptional sense of smell.
They help keep ecosystems healthy by removing dead animals.

name: Mountain Lion
ability: Challenge any Organism.
If this wins a Challenge, the defeated Organism is moved to the Compost.
fact: Also called cougar, puma, or panther.
Can leap over 40 feet in a single bound.

name: Bobcat
ability: Challenge any Organism.
If this wins a Challenge, refresh this Organism.
fact: The bobbed tail gives the bobcat its name.
An adaptable predator found throughout Arizona.

name: Great Horned Owl
ability: Challenge any Organism.
May Challenge immediately after entering play.
fact: A powerful nocturnal hunter.
Can prey on animals larger than itself.

name: Pinto Creek
ability: Provides 1 Water Energy.
Exhaust to pay Water costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Pinto Creek A 28-mile tributary that winds northward from the Pinal Mountains through the Sonoran Desert, emptying directly into the eastern "tail" of Roosevelt Lake.

name: Parker Creek
ability: Provides 1 Water Energy.
Exhaust to pay Water costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Parker Creek A seasonal stream flowing out of the Sierra Ancha Wilderness that feeds into the northeastern side of the reservoir.

name: Bird Eggs
ability: Provides 1 Fauna Energy.
Exhaust to pay Fauna costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Many Sonoran Desert animals eat bird eggs when the opportunity arises.

name: Round-tailed Ground Squirrel
ability: Provides 1 Fauna Energy.
Exhaust to pay Fauna costs.
Refresh at the start of your turn.
fact: Common throughout the Sonoran Desert.
Eats seeds, grasses, leaves, and occasional insects.
Provides an important food source for hawks, owls, snakes, foxes, bobcats, and coyotes.

name: Monsoon Storm
ability: Restore 1 Water Energy to all Habitat cards.
All Habitat Flora gain +1 Challenge Rank until the start of your next turn.
fact: The North American Monsoon brings much of Arizona's annual rainfall during the summer.
Monsoon storms can produce intense rain, lightning, strong winds, and flash floods.
Many Sonoran Desert plants and animals rely on seasonal monsoon rains.
